metadata.opportunity: Aurora MySQL to distributed SQL modernization

turns.text: We are on AWS Aurora MySQL and our primary order history table is around 50TB logical, growing about 1.3TB per month.
Peak traffic is roughly 11k writes per second and 35k reads per second during promo windows. Replica lag is a recurring issue.
Helpful context. Are you evaluating this mainly for scale, availability, or cost pressure compared to current Aurora spend?
All three. We have weekly schema changes and online ALTER TABLE on large partitions is operationally painful and risky.
For next call we should map your top query classes, ETL windows, and strict p95 latency targets so we can shape architecture options.
Perfect. We also need cross-region recovery and lower operational toil during peak season.
